whenAll: all four gates plus design-integrity pass on openchrome re-run
source-apply transaction records rollback and audit refs
thin human go/no-go required after source-apply and trust-gradient evidence
proposal stays queued and unapplied until the high-risk tail is approved
failed or missing four-gate/design-integrity re-verify evidence
missing rollback or stale source-apply evidence

boundary: M71 self-improvement routing contract reuses openchrome, scenario verdicts, the four gates plus design-integrity, verdict.json, journal.md, ledger.jsonl, loop-coverage attribution, source-apply, trust-gradient, rollback, and kill-switch artifacts; no new verification engine and no new data plane; low-risk reversible fixes can route to bounded auto-apply through existing source-apply/trust-gradient, while high-risk/source-affecting changes remain queued for a thin human go/no-go; fun/taste and release go/no-go remain human Ring 2; #1 and #23 remain open.
guardrails: The contract specifies routing only; it does not execute openchrome, mutate source, merge branches, or create a verifier.
Source-affecting/high-risk changes never auto-apply and remain queued for thin human go/no-go.
Rollback and kill-switch are required existing artifacts for any apply route.
